title: Metridium garden
body: The future is not determined by what we can extract — but by what we choose to act on now.

seabedMining.heading: Seabed Mining Matters
seabedMining.subheading: But is NOT directly relevant to our scope of action.
seabedMining.highlight: Seabed mining / its prevention is not our mission. It is a very clear boundary case.
seabedMining.body: The deep seabed is slow-forming, globally shared, minimally understood, and one of the least polluted, destroyed, or humanly touched environments on Earth. Proposed industrial activity in this domain exposes a broader pattern: material throughput is prioritized over interaction quality, while technologies are deployed faster than our ability to measure, govern, or reverse their effects.
seabedMining.detail: If we cannot interact safely with the most fragile and least resilient systems on the planet, confidence in our practices elsewhere should be questioned. Seabed preservation therefore functions as a reference point, an opportunity — not a limitation — for developing universally aligned innovation pathways across sectors.
